patch.before: AEO is how you get your brand quoted—not just ranked—when someone asks ChatGPT a question about your category. Unlike traditional SEO, AEO targets how large language models select, quote, and reference sources to respond directly to user questions. patch.after: AEO is how you get your brand quoted, not just ranked, when someone asks ChatGPT a question about your category. patch.before: Answer engine optimization is a process purpose-built for maximizing citation and visibility in AI answer engines as opposed to human-centric SERPs. The winners in AEO aren't the loudest—they're the most quotable. Write one sentence an LLM can steal cleanly.[geo-arxiv][semrush-aeo-2024]
patch.after: Answer engine optimization is a process purpose-built for maximizing citation and visibility in AI answer engines as opposed to human-centric SERPs. patch.before: Princeton's 2023 GEO study (arXiv 2311.09735) found citations boosted visibility 30-40% and statistics added another 25% on Perplexity specifically. Additionally, structured schema.org elements, especially DefinedTerm, improve definition and concept clarity for AI engines. E-E-A-T is mostly theater for AEO—LLMs don't read your author bios; they quote whoever structured the sentence most cleanly.[geo-arxiv][schema-org-defined-term][google-search-central]
patch.after: Princeton's 2023 GEO study (arXiv 2311.09735) found citations boosted visibility 30-40% and statistics added another 25% on Perplexity specifically. patch.before: Most AEO advice is garbage—schema markup alone won't get you cited; what actually works is being the primary source others already link to. definitional posts should have DefinedTerm schema — not present

Author has no jobTitle or description — credentials not stated

Genuine opinions vs safe claims 6/10 — A few sharp takes (E-E-A-T is theater, most AEO advice is garbage) exist but are surrounded by hedged, generic claims.

specificity 5/10 — The post names tools and schema types well, but several sentences float in abstractions — 'authority,' 'visibility,' 'relevance,' 'signals' — without attaching numbers, named case studies, or concrete benchmarks. Phrases like 'organizations can ensure continuous brand relevance' and 'gain citation share, authority, and visibility' are vague assertions. The post cites Semrush and arXiv papers in brackets but never quotes a single specific finding, statistic, or example from them, which undermines the specificity it promises.
